evals.prompt: I have a research paper (paper.tex) and a Python codebase in this project. Please generate a comprehensive GitHub README.md for this repository.
evals.expected_output: A clean, developer-first README (~150-300 lines) with verified badges, no hallucinated content, no HTML wrappers, and installation coming before methodology.
expectations: README.md is between 150-400 lines (not too short, not too long)
Badge information is verifiable from actual files: Python version from setup.py, License from LICENSE, arXiv from .bib
No hallucinated badges (e.g., code-style:black when no black config exists)
No HTML wrappers like <div align='center'>
No raw LaTeX commands in prose (no \begin{...}, \cite{...}, \ref{...}, \label{...})
Equations use $$...$$ display blocks on their own line — never $...$ inline math (Typora compatibility)
No spacing tweaks (\!, \,, \quad, \qquad) in equations
Every equation block is followed by a plain-language explanation sentence
Installation section comes before Methodology section (developer-first ordering)
Installation commands are derived from actual dependency files (pip install -r requirements.txt)
No invented content: no virtual env instructions, no docker commands unless they exist in the project
No redundancy: no separate dependency table if requirements.txt is already shown, no separate Reproducibility section duplicating training commands
Citation BibTeX is the exact entry from references.bib (smith2025hiergat), not reconstructed from LaTeX metadata
arXiv badge uses the actual arXiv ID (2503.12345) from the .bib file, not a placeholder

evals.prompt: Please generate a Chinese (中文) README for this project.
evals.expected_output: A Chinese README where technical descriptions flow naturally in Chinese, code blocks and BibTeX remain in English, and the translation reads like native technical Chinese, not word-for-word translation.
expectations: README.md descriptive text is primarily in Chinese
Code blocks, terminal commands, and BibTeX entries are preserved in English
Chinese translation is meaning-based, not word-for-word (e.g., '自底向上的注意力机制' not '底向上注意机制')
Technical terms are correctly translated to standard Chinese ML terminology
No English sentences mixed into Chinese paragraphs (occasional English technical terms like 'HierGAT' are acceptable)
Citation BibTeX is preserved exactly as-is in English
Equations use $$...$$ blocks with clean LaTeX (no spacing tweaks) and have plain-language explanations

evals.prompt: Generate a minimal/short README for this research code repository — just the essentials.
evals.expected_output: A compact README under 150 lines with only: title, badges, overview, installation, quick start, citation. No methodology, no results tables, no project structure.
expectations: README.md is under 150 lines
Includes: title, abstract/overview, installation, quick start, citation
Does NOT include detailed methodology section
Does NOT include results tables or experiment details
All badge URLs are complete (no empty parentheses like '()')
Citation BibTeX is correct and complete
